source: NYU Admissions — Course Equivalencies for Internal Transfers to Stern (lines 17-39 + Calculus / Regression / Microeconomics / Financial Accounting sections). GPA caveat per ARCHITECTURE.md §1604.
triggerQueries: what cas course satisfies stern calc
what cas course satisfies stern micro
body:
**Internal transfer prereqs by entry year — verbatim from NYU's Course Equivalencies for Internal Transfers to Stern page:**

*"If you wish to transfer into the sophomore year at Stern, you should have completed the following:"* — Calculus I, II, or III + Writing/Composition (1-2 semesters).

*"If you wish to transfer into the junior year at Stern, you should have completed following:"* — Calculus I, II, or III + Writing/Composition + Microeconomics + Regression + Financial Accounting.

*"Completion of these courses with letter grades (no pass/fail permitted) is required for the application. There is no guarantee that all previously taken coursework or advanced standing credit will count toward Stern degree requirements."*

## Which CAS courses satisfy each prereq

**Writing and Composition** — *"Courses that fulfill “Writing and Composition” prerequisite are equivalent to your current NYU college’s writing requirement."* For CAS students, this is EXPOS-UA 1 (or equivalent).

**Calculus I, II, or III** — any one of:
- MATH-UA 121 Calculus I (MATH-UA 9121 if taken abroad)
- MATH-UA 122 Calculus II (MATH-UA 9122 if taken abroad)
- MATH-UA 123 Calculus III (MATH-UA 9123 if taken abroad)
- MATH-UA 132 Math for Economics II or higher
- MATH-UA 221 Honors Calculus I
- AP Calculus AB score of 4 or 5

**Regression** (junior-entry only) — for CAS students:
- ECON-UA 380 Topics in Econometrics
- ECON-UA 266 Introduction to Econometrics

**Microeconomics** (junior-entry only) — for CAS students:
- ECON-UA 2 Introduction to Microeconomics
- ECON-UA 10 (or 9010) Intermediate Microeconomics
- ECON-UA 11 Microeconomic Analysis

**Financial Accounting** (junior-entry only) — Stern's ACCT-UB 1 / 9001 are accepted; no CAS equivalent is listed on the page, so a CAS student typically takes the Stern course directly (with permission) or completes it after admission. Confirm with Stern admissions.

## Application logistics (sourced from the Stern bulletin §Internal Transfer Applicants — see also `stern_residency` template for verbatim quotes)

- **Application deadline:** by the March 1 application deadline. Stern accepts transfers in the fall only.
- **Minimum credits:** complete (or be in the process of completing) one full year (32 credits) of academic study in your original school of enrollment.
- **Disqualifier:** Stern does not consider applicants who previously externally transferred to another school or college at NYU.
- **Important caveat — GPA thresholds are not published.** The Stern bulletin does not specify a numeric minimum GPA for internal transfer. Strong applicants typically present competitive grades, but no public floor exists. **Confirm any GPA expectations directly with Stern Admissions before applying.**

*Some of these courses may have prerequisites for enrollment.*
